threads.description: The Action Chain system replaces hard‑coded Rust pipelines with data‑driven YAML chains that build hierarchical pyramid nodes. It defines a runtime composed of six Rust modules, a rich chain schema, variable resolution, multiple step execution modes, resume support, and error handling, while exposing model tier configuration and a workflow for custom variants. Default chains exist for conversation, code, and document pipelines; future work adds vine, delta, meta chains, Wire sync, and a UI editor.
threads.name: Action Chain System

threads.description: The document defines the Wire's Machine Layer architecture (River, Graph, Machine) and the Compiler that turns declarative action chains into executable intelligence. It specifies contribution types, operation primitives, economics, trust model, and how machines can fork, evolve, and self‑extend via the Build Market.
threads.name: Agent Wire Compiler Architecture

threads.description: The document defines the AI‑operated newsroom "Good News Everyone", describing how a Supabase‑backed dataset is written by a decoupled pipeline and read by the reader/admin UI. It lists the canonical pipeline stages, their locking semantics, the scheduler that drives them, and the security model for API/cron access.
threads.name: Good News Everyone Architecture

threads.description: The v4.2 plan extends the v3 delta‑chain with automatic pyramid freshness, wiring dead code, adding per‑layer timers, and full LLM‑driven stale checks. It defines new schema, a write‑ahead log, and runtime components (file watcher, debounce timers, helper dispatch) while introducing breaker/freeze UI, cost observability, and an FAQ‑generalization engine. The document also lists the implementation order and known bugs to fix before Phase 4.
threads.name: Auto‑Stale System Implementation

threads.description: The document defines a feedback loop for the Pyramid knowledge base: every agent can write annotations that automatically update FAQ edges and usage logs, turning the Pyramid into a living document. It outlines three core subsystems, their data flow, storage schema, API surface, and a phased rollout plan that integrates with the Conductor orchestrator.
threads.name: Contributions‑Back System

threads.description: The document defines the relational schema for a multi‑user, AI‑driven news platform. It emphasizes per‑user isolation, JSONB flexibility, full cost transparency, and temporal tracking, while mapping the end‑to‑end content pipeline from ingestion to reader interaction. The model also details operational controls such as model slot assignments, cost logging, full‑text search, and row‑level security policies.
threads.name: AI Newsroom Data Model

threads.description: The audit of the Delta‑Chain architecture (2026‑03‑22) uncovered 4 critical, 12 major and ~10 minor issues. The document lists mandatory schema changes, concurrency fixes, prompt redesigns, and operational safeguards that must be applied before further implementation.
threads.name: Delta‑Chain Audit Corrections
